claim_status: IPC readiness-review template only; no owner review, no security review, no architecture review, no API review, no performance review, no implemented schema generator, no approved generator source, no wire encoding decision, no generated types, no generated validators, no generated fixtures, no connection authentication review, no bounded queues review, no backpressure review, no timeout semantics review, no cancellation semantics review, no stale-epoch receiver proof, no negative-test review, no fuzz or model-test review, no process-capability generation review, no PB-011 readiness promotion, no renderer-security claim, no agent-security claim, no process-isolation readiness claim, no site-isolation claim, no production IPC claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not leave null fields in a real IPC readiness review or name placeholder boundary inventories, schema-source templates, schema generators, wire encoding decisions, owner reviewers, security reviewers, architecture reviewers, API reviewers, performance reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, or implied PB-011 readiness authority.

schema_generator_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed implemented schema generator evidence beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template and checked no-claim schema-source template.
Architecture and security review approve the generator source, regeneration command, generated-output ownership, and source-of-truth policy beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template.
Generated request, response, event, error, and handle types are reviewed against process-capability records and schema-source metadata.
Generated validators enforce size, count, role, capability, principal, profile, origin, top-level site, document epoch, and resource-owner constraints before receiver dispatch.
Generated protocol documentation records versioning, compatibility, error codes, redaction, trace, audit, unknown variant policy, and compatibility fixture paths.
Compatibility fixtures cover forward, backward, optional-field, required-field, unknown variant, and schema-diff behavior.
Negative fixtures exist for malformed, oversized, stale, duplicate, reordered, unauthorized, wrong-principal, timeout, and cancellation cases.
Decoder and state-machine fuzz corpus seeds are generated or reviewed for trust-boundary coverage.
Schema-diff classification blocks authority widening, persistent-format changes, and incompatible wire changes without owner review.

wire_transport_axes.required_evidence: Reviewed wire encoding decision proves bounded decode behavior, unknown variant handling, versioning, fuzzability, and compatibility beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template.
Decoder rejects malformed tags, lengths, counts, enum values, offsets, handles, and nested structures before large allocation or privileged dispatch.
Unknown required variants fail closed and unknown optional fields have documented compatibility behavior.
Privileged receivers authenticate process identity, role, profile, principal, and kernel-issued process state before accepting messages.
Every trust-boundary queue declares capacity, overload behavior, coalescing, cancellation priority, and termination behavior.
Backpressure tests prove overload fails closed without unbounded memory, hidden wakeups, or control-message starvation.
Timeout semantics cover queued work, in-flight work, partial effects, process crash, cleanup reporting, and stable timeout errors.
Cancellation semantics cover not-started, stopped-before-effect, stopped-after-partial-effect, too-late-after-commit, cleanup, and grant revocation.
Document-scoped receivers reject stale document epochs after navigation, crash, BFCache, timeout, cancellation, or asynchronous delay.

authority_identity_axes.required_evidence: Browser kernel messages recompute privileged authority against trusted state and never accept page-provided authority.
Renderer-originating messages carry assigned IPC authority only and fail closed on ambient network, filesystem, credential, debug, device, update, or internal-protocol expansion.
Network messages stay scoped to kernel-issued request context and cannot read password vaults or arbitrary profile files.
Storage messages use logical profile and origin handles without ambient network, credential export, UI, or process-creation authority.
GPU messages validate surfaces, shared memory, resource sizes, and ownership before device or memory use.
Media utility messages are task scoped with bounded hostile input and output and no ambient profile, network, UI, credential, or arbitrary filesystem authority.
Extension messages bind declared APIs, host grants, profile/incognito policy, and quotas without kernel-memory, ungranted-profile, native-messaging, update-signing, or agent-confirmation authority.
DevTools messages bind an attached target, visible authorization state, target epoch, and remote-debug policy without unattached-profile or signing authority.

Agent messages carry granted observation/action scope, document epoch, provider timeout/cancellation, confirmation state, and audit metadata without raw cookies, password vault, direct renderer memory, direct input injection, confirmation approval, ungranted files, or ungranted origins.
Updater messages stay scoped to signed metadata, staging, and version roots without profile content, browser history, agent data, or arbitrary execution authority.
Crash-handler messages use bounded dump channels, redaction, consent, build-symbol identity, and no ambient profile read or browser command authority.
Process-capability records generate or validate per-message required capability metadata so crate behavior, registries, and docs cannot drift.

negative_test_axes.required_evidence: Malformed encoding fixtures and fuzz cases are rejected before allocation, receiver dispatch, or privileged side effects.
Oversized size, count, queue, and shared-memory cases are rejected across generated message families and stream paths.
Stale document epoch and expired capability cases are rejected after navigation, crash, BFCache, timeout, and asynchronous delay.
Duplicate request identifiers, sequence numbers, and idempotency keys do not replay privileged effects.
Out-of-order stream and subscription cases fail, resynchronize, or emit explicit gap events without hidden state corruption.
Senders without required role, capability, profile, origin, site, document epoch, or policy state are rejected.
Cross-profile, cross-origin, wrong-role, stale-process, extension, DevTools, and agent action attempts are rejected without leaking target state.
Timeout tests cover queued work, in-flight work, partial effects, process crash, network or storage wait, cleanup reporting, and no hidden retries.
Cancellation tests cover not-started, stopped-before-effect, stopped-after-partial-effect, too-late-after-commit, cleanup, and grant revocation.
Decoder, queue, state-machine, stale-epoch, duplicate, reordering, authorization, timeout, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion behavior have fuzz, property, or model tests.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Owner accepts the boundary inventory, schema-source replacement, schema generator, wire encoding, authority model, negative tests, and PB-011 status change beyond the checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template.
Security reviewer accepts confused-deputy, wrong-principal, stale-epoch, capability, connection-authentication, redaction, and audit behavior.
Architecture reviewer accepts process topology, schema family ownership, generated-output ownership, and trust-boundary queue behavior.
API reviewer accepts versioning, unknown fields, error codes, compatibility fixtures, and schema-diff policy.
Performance reviewer accepts maximum encoded sizes, queue bounds, allocation bounds, backpressure, resource attribution, and overload behavior.
Privacy reviewer accepts sensitivity, redaction, trace, audit, crash, and provider-visible IPC metadata handling.
Pre-build readiness, task queue, research crosswalk, operating board, repository map, API design, security docs, and research index are updated after review.
Owner approval records expiry, schema family scope, process roles, source commit, generated-output scope, and stale-evidence rerun policy.
PB-011 readiness promotion remains separate from renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, implementation, and Chrome-class approval.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim IPC readiness-review template is cited as owner-reviewed IPC readiness, PB-011 readiness, renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, or implementation evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject PB-011 readiness, renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Boundary inventory, schema-source template, schema generator, wire encoding decision, owner reviewer, security reviewer, architecture reviewer, API reviewer, or performance reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and IPC readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: No implemented schema generator, approved generator source, regeneration command, generated-output ownership, or source-of-truth policy exists.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject schema-generator approval, generated type, generated validator, generated fixture, and PB-011 readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Wire encoding decision, bounded decode behavior, unknown variant policy, compatibility behavior, or fuzzability evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject production IPC and protocol stability claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Generated validators do not enforce size, count, role, capability, principal, profile, origin, top-level site, document epoch, and resource-owner constraints before receiver dispatch.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, and production IPC claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Negative tests for malformed, oversized, stale, duplicate, reordered, unauthorized, wrong-principal, timeout, cancellation, fuzz, or model behavior are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject PB-011 readiness and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Page content, DevTools, extension, agent, renderer, model output, or untrusted IPC input can mint capabilities or widen authority beyond kernel-issued state.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject all security and production IPC claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Privileged receivers do not authenticate sender process identity, role, profile, principal, capability, and kernel-issued process state.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject wrong-principal, process-isolation, site-isolation, and production IPC claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Trust-boundary queues, streams, shared memory, or task dispatch can grow without explicit bounds, backpressure, overload behavior, or termination policy.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject performance, reliability, and production IPC claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Timeout or cancellation behavior lacks deadlines, partial-effect reporting, cleanup, grant revocation, or stable result state.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject timeout/cancellation implementation and PB-011 readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Receivers do not reject stale document epochs, stale processes, expired capabilities, or asynchronous work after navigation, crash, BFCache, timeout, or cancellation.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject stale-epoch, renderer-security, agent-security, and site-isolation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Generated schemas, crates, process-capability records, docs, and tests disagree on required capability, forbidden authority, process role, or principal identity.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and PB-011 readiness claims until the sources are reconciled.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing validation or M0 crate tests is cited as renderer-security, agent-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, implementation, release, or Chrome-class evidence.
A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, no-claim wording, claim boundary wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement owner-reviewed IPC evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-011 partial.
